volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 881 - Section 981)

sections.label: Section 881
sections.original: ὦ τᾶς ἑπταφθόγγου μέλπων
sections.translation: O ! thou who dost awake that tuneful lyre with seven strings till to its sweet note of music the lifeless pegs of wild ox-hom resound again ,

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: σοὶ μομφάν , ὦ Λατοῦς παῖ ,
sections.translation: thou child of Latona , to yon 293 bright orb of thine will I publish thy reproach . Yes , I saw thee come , the glint of gold upon thy locks , as I was gathering in my folded robe the saffron blooms

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: ἀνθίζειν χρυσανταυγῆ ·
sections.translation: that blazed like flowers of gold ; and by my lily wrist didst thou catch me and ledst me to the cavern ’ s bed , what time I cried aloud upon my mother ’ s name ,— thou a god

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: ἆγες ἀναιδείᾳ
sections.translation: to mate with me in shameless wise to pleasure lady Cypris ! Then to my sorrow I bore thee a son , whom , though anguish thrilled my mother ’ s breast , I cast upon that bed of thine ,

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: ἵνα με λέχεσι μελέαν μελέοις
sections.translation: where thou didst join in woful wedlock this unhappy maid . Ah ! woe is me ! that poor babe I bare thee is now no more ; winged fowls have tom and devoured him ,

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: σὺ δὲ κιθάρᾳ κλάζεις
sections.translation: but thou art gaily carolling unto thy lyre some song of joy .

sections.label: Section 907
sections.original: ὠή ,
sections.translation: Hark ! thou son of Latona , to thee I call , for that thou dispensest warnings ; there at thy golden throne

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: καὶ γαίας μεσσήρεις ἕδρας ,
sections.translation: on earth ’ s centre planted will I proclaim a word into thy ear . O ! thou wicked bridegroom who art bringing to my husband ’ s house an heir , though from him thou hast received no boon ;

sections.label: Section 916
sections.original: ὁ δʼ ἐμὸς γενέτας καὶ σός γʼ , ἀμαθής ,
sections.translation: while that child of thine and mine hath died unrecognized , a prey to carrion birds , his mother ’ s swaddling-clothes all lost . Delos hates thee now , thy bay-tree loves thee not ,

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ἔρνεα φοίνικα παρʼ ἁβροκόμαν ,
sections.translation: whose branches sprout beside the tufted palm , where in holy throes Latona , big with child by Zeus , gave birth to thee .

sections.label: Section 923
sections.original: οἴμοι , μέγας θησαυρὸς ὡς ἀνοίγνυται
sections.translation: Ah me ! what store of sorrows is here disclosed , enough to draw a tear from every eye !

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , οὔτοι σὸν βλέπων ἐμπίμπλαμαι
sections.translation: Daughter , with pity am I filled as a gaze upon thy face ; my reason leaves me ; for just as I am striving to lighten my spirit of its sea of troubles , comes another wave astern and catches me by reason of thy words ; for no sooner hadst thou uttered this tale of present troubles

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: μετῆλθες ἄλλων πημάτων κακὰς ὁδούς .
sections.translation: than thou didst turn aside into a fresh a track of other woes . What is it thou sayest ? What charge against Apollo dost thou bring ? What child is this thou dost assert that thou didst bear ? Where was it in the city that thou didst expose him , for beasts to rejoice o ’ er his burial ? Tell me once again .

sections.label: Section 934
sections.original: αἰσχύνομαι μέν σʼ , ὦ γέρον , λέξω δʼ ὅμως .
sections.translation: Old friend , although to meet thine eye , I am ashamed , yet will I tell thee .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ὡς συστενάζειν γʼ οἶδα γενναίως φίλοις .
sections.translation: Full well I know how to lend my friends a generous sympathy .

sections.label: Section 936
sections.original: ἄκουε τοίνυν · οἶσθα Κεκροπίας πέτρας
sections.translation: Then hearken ; dost know a cave toward the north of Cecrops ’ rock , that we call Macrae ?

sections.label: Section 938
sections.original: οἶδʼ , ἔνθα Πανὸς ἄδυτα καὶ βωμοὶ πέλας .
sections.translation: I know it ; there is the shrine of Pan , and his altar hard by .

sections.label: Section 939
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ ἀγῶνα δεινὸν ἠγωνίσμεθα .
sections.translation: That was the scene of my dire conflict .

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: τίνʼ ; ὡς ἀπαντᾷ δάκρυά μοι τοῖς σοῖς λόγοις .
sections.translation: What conflict ? see how my tears start forth to meet thy words .

sections.label: Section 941
sections.original: Φοίβῳ ξυνῆψʼ ἄκουσα δύστηνον γάμον .
sections.translation: Phoebus forced me to a woful marriage .

sections.label: Section 942
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , ἆρʼ ἦν ταῦθʼ ἅ γʼ ᾐσθόμην ἐγώ ;
sections.translation: Was it then this , my daughter , that I noticed myself ?

sections.label: Section 943
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἀληθῆ δʼ εἰ λέγεις φαίημεν ἄν .
sections.translation: I know not ; but I will tell thee if thou speak the truth .

sections.label: Section 944
sections.original: νόσον κρυφαίαν ἡνίκʼ ἔστενες λάθρα ;
sections.translation: At the time thou wert mourning in secret some hidden complaint ?

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: τότʼ ἦν ἃ νῦν σοι φανερὰ σημαίνω κακά .
sections.translation: Yes , ’ twas then this trouble happened , which now I am declaring to thee .

sections.label: Section 946
sections.original: κᾆτʼ ἐξέκλεψας πῶς Ἀπόλλωνος γάμους ;
sections.translation: How then didst conceal thy union with Apollo ?

sections.label: Section 947
sections.original: ἔτεκον — ἀνάσχου ταῦτʼ ἐμοῦ κλύων , γέρον .
sections.translation: I bore a child ; hear me patiently , old friend .

sections.label: Section 948
sections.original: ποῦ ; τίς λοχεύει σʼ ; ἢ μόνη μοχθεῖς τάδε ;
sections.translation: Where ? and who helped thy travail ? or didst thou labour all alone ?

sections.label: Section 949
sections.original: μόνη κατʼ ἄντρον οὗπερ ἐζεύχθην γάμοις .
sections.translation: All alone , in the cave where I became a wife .

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: ὁ παῖς δὲ ποῦ ʼστιν ; ἵνα σὺ μηκέτʼ ᾖς ἄπαις .
sections.translation: Where is the child ? that thou mayst cease thy childless state .

sections.label: Section 951
sections.original: τέθνηκεν , ὦ γεραιέ , θηρσὶν ἐκτεθείς .
sections.translation: Dead , old friend , to beasts exposed .

sections.label: Section 952
sections.original: τέθνηκʼ ; Ἀπόλλων δʼ ὁ κακὸς οὐδὲν ἤρκεσεν ;
sections.translation: Dead ? did Apollo , evil god , no help afford ?

sections.label: Section 953
sections.original: οὐκ ἤρκεσʼ · Ἅιδου δʼ ἐν δόμοις παιδεύεται .
sections.translation: None ; my boy is in the halls of Hades .

sections.label: Section 954
sections.original: τίς γάρ νιν ἐξέθηκεν ; οὐ γὰρ δὴ σύ γε .
sections.translation: Who then exposed him ? surely not thyself .

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: ἡμεῖς , ἐν ὄρφνῃ σπαργανώσαντες πέπλοις .
sections.translation: Myself , when ’ neath the gloom of night I had wrapped him in my robe .

sections.label: Section 956
sections.original: οὐδὲ ξυνῄδει σοί τις ἔκθεσιν τέκνου ;
sections.translation: Did no one share thy secret of the babe ’ s exposure ?

sections.label: Section 957
sections.original: αἱ ξυμφοραί γε καὶ τὸ λανθάνειν μόνον .
sections.translation: Ill-fortune and secrecy alone .

sections.label: Section 958
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ἐν ἄντρῳ παῖδα σὸν λιπεῖν ἔτλης ;
sections.translation: How couldst thou in the cavern leave thy babe ?

sections.label: Section 959
sections.original: πῶς δʼ οἰκτρὰ πολλὰ στόματος ἐκβαλοῦσʼ ἔπη —
sections.translation: Ah ! how ? but still I did , with many a word of pity uttered o ’ er him .

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Oh for thy hard heart ! Oh for the god ’ s , more hard than thine !

sections.label: Section 961
sections.original: εἰ παῖδά γʼ εἶδες χεῖρας ἐκτείνοντά μοι .
sections.translation: Hadst thou but seen the babe stretch forth his hands to me !

sections.label: Section 962
sections.original: μαστὸν διώκοντʼ ἦ πρὸς ἀγκάλαις πεσεῖν ;
sections.translation: To find thy mother ’ s breast , to nestle in thy arms ?

sections.label: Section 963
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ , ἵνʼ οὐκ ὢν ἄδικʼ ἔπασχεν ἐξ ἐμοῦ .
sections.translation: By being kept therefrom he suffered grievous wrong from me .

sections.label: Section 964
sections.original: σοὶ δʼ ἐς τί δόξʼ ἐσῆλθεν ἐκβαλεῖν τέκνον ;
sections.translation: How earnest thou to think of casting forth thy babe ?

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: ὡς τὸν θεὸν σῴζοντα τόν γʼ αὑτοῦ γόνον .
sections.translation: Methought the god would save his own begotten child .

sections.label: Section 966
sections.original: οἴμοι , δόμων σῶν ὄλβος ὡς χειμάζεται .
sections.translation: Ah me ! what storms assail thy family ’ s prosperity !

sections.label: Section 967
sections.original: τί κρᾶτα κρύψας , ὦ γέρον , δακρυρροεῖς ;
sections.translation: Why weepest thou , old man , with head close-veiled ?

sections.label: Section 968
sections.original: σὲ καὶ πατέρα σὸν δυστυχοῦντας εἰσορῶ .
sections.translation: To see the sorrows of thy sire and thee .

sections.label: Section 969
sections.original: τὰ θνητὰ τοιαῦτʼ · οὐδὲν ἐν ταὐτῷ μένει .
sections.translation: Such is our mortal life ; naught abideth in one stay .

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: μή νυν ἔτʼ οἴκτων , θύγατερ , ἀντεχώμεθα .
sections.translation: Daughter , let us cease to dwell on themes of woe .

sections.label: Section 971
sections.original: τί γάρ με χρὴ δρᾶν ; ἀπορία τὸ δυστυχεῖν .
sections.translation: What must I do ? Misfortune leaves us helpless .

sections.label: Section 972
sections.original: τὸν πρῶτον ἀδικήσαντά σʼ ἀποτίνου θεόν .
sections.translation: Avenge thee on the god who first did injure thee .

sections.label: Section 973
sections.original: καὶ πῶς τὰ κρείσσω θνητὸς οὖσʼ ὑπερδράμω ;
sections.translation: How can I , weak mortal as I am , outrun those mightier powers ?

sections.label: Section 974
sections.original: πίμπρη τὰ σεμνὰ Λοξίου χρηστήρια .
sections.translation: Set fire to Apollo ’ s awful sanctuary .

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: δέδοικα · καὶ νῦν πημάτων ἄδην ἔχω .
sections.translation: I am afraid ; my present sorrows are enough for me .

sections.label: Section 976
sections.original: τὰ δυνατά νυν τόλμησον , ἄνδρα σὸν κτανεῖν .
sections.translation: Then what thou canst , that dare — thy husband ’ s death .

sections.label: Section 977
sections.original: αἰδούμεθʼ εὐνὰς τὰς τόθʼ ἡνίκʼ ἐσθλὸς ἦν .
sections.translation: Nay , I do respect his former love in the days when he was good and true .

sections.label: Section 978
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ἀλλὰ παῖδα τὸν ἐπὶ σοὶ πεφηνότα .
sections.translation: At least , then , slay the boy who hath appeared to supplant thee .

sections.label: Section 979
sections.original: πῶς ; εἰ γὰρ εἴη δυνατόν · ὡς θέλοιμί γʼ ἄν .
sections.translation: How can I ? would it were possible ! how I wish it were !

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: ξιφηφόρους σοὺς ὁπλίσασʼ ὀπάονας .
sections.translation: Arm thy followers with daggers .

sections.label: Section 981
sections.original: στείχοιμʼ ἄν · ἀλλὰ ποῦ γενήσεται τόδε ;
sections.translation: I will about it ; but where is the deed to be done ?
